name: Villa del Prado, Pelayos de la Presa,
name_original: Villa del Prado, Pelayos de la Presa, Aldea del Fresno – ruta circular
description: This 40.6-mile (65.4 km) difficult road cycling route through Villa del Prado, Pelayos de la Presa, and Aldea del Fresno offers scenic views
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Villa Del Prado offer diverse landscapes, ranging from flat to rolling terrain with manageable hills. The region is characterized by the Sierra Oeste and Guadarrama Mountains, providing scenic backdrops and varied elevations. Cyclists can explore paths through pine forests and along the Alberche River, which is dammed by the Picadas dam, creating reservoir views. This area provides a mix of challenging routes and more gentle sections, suitable for different road cycling preferences. Best road cycling routes around Villa Del Prado The most popular road cycling route is Villa del Prado, Pelayos de la Presa, a 40.6 miles (65.4 km) trail that takes about 3 hours 10 minutes to complete. This difficult route passes through areas known for pine forests and reservoir proximity. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Coracera Castle – Río Alberche loop from Urb. Calalberche - Rio Manzanares, a difficult 52.9 miles (85.1 km) path. This route features the Alberche River and views of Coracera Castle. Local road cyclists also love the Castle of la Coracera – Burguillo Reservoir loop from Pelayos de la Presa, a 71.6 miles (115.2 km) trail leading through areas near Pelayos de la Presa and Burguillo Reservoir, often completed in about 5 hours 54 minutes. Road cycling around Villa Del Prado is defined by the Sierra Oeste mountains, the Alberche River, and pine forests. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various skill levels. The routes in Villa Del Prado are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 2000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Villa Del Prado's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Villa Del Prado?
A: There are over 80 road cycling routes available around Villa Del Prado, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Villa Del Prado?
A: Road cycling routes in Villa Del Prado feature diverse landscapes, from flat to rolling terrain with manageable hills. The region is characterized by the Sierra Oeste and Guadarrama Mountains, providing scenic backdrops and varied elevations. You'll find paths through pine forests and along the Alberche River, often with reservoir views.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Villa Del Prado?
A: Yes, Villa Del Prado offers routes suitable for various skill levels. While many routes are challenging, there are 8 easy routes and 42 moderate routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5313835" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alberche Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, built on an old railway line, provides a delightful and generally favorable path through a canyon, which can be a good option for less experienced riders or families.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions to see while road cycling in Villa Del Prado?
A: While road cycling, you can enjoy the natural beauty of the region, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5313835" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alberche Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5662891" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picadas Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2885390" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Juan Reservoir Marina&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also pass through beautiful pine forests, particularly around Pelayos de la Presa.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, some routes offer glimpses of historical landmarks. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31916987" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coracera Castle – Río Alberche loop&lt;/a&gt; features views of Coracera Castle. Another route, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40319144" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Escalona Castle Palace – Main Square of Pelahustán loop&lt;/a&gt;, passes by the Escalona Castle Palace.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Villa Del Prado?
A: The road cycling routes in Villa Del Prado are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 2000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the scenic views and diverse challenges offered by the Sierra Oeste mountains and river landscapes.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, Villa Del Prado offers several long-distance road cycling routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40646089" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of la Coracera – Burguillo Reservoir loop from Pelayos de la Presa&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 115.2 km (71.6 miles) trail, often completed in about 6 hours.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes around Villa Del Prado?
A: Many of the road cycling routes in Villa Del Prado are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31916987" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coracera Castle – Río Alberche loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40646089" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of la Coracera – Burguillo Reservoir loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are the typical weather conditions for road cycling in Villa Del Prado?
A: Villa Del Prado, located in the Community of Madrid, generally experiences a Mediterranean climate with hot, dry summers and mild winters. Spring and autumn typically offer pleasant temperatures, making them ideal for road cycling. It's always advisable to check the local forecast before heading out.

Q: Are there opportunities for other outdoor activities near the road cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. Beyond road cycling, the region offers a range of outdoor activities. The nearby &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2885390" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Juan Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is excellent for watersports like kayaking and paddleboarding. The rugged terrain and natural parks also provide numerous hiking opportunities, and birdwatching is popular due to the rich biodiversity.

Q: What is the difficulty level of most road cycling routes in Villa Del Prado?
A: The road cycling routes around Villa Del Prado cater to various skill levels. Out of 88 routes, 8 are easy, 42 are moderate, and 38 are difficult. This means there are plenty of challenging options for experienced riders, as well as more accessible routes for those seeking a less strenuous ride.
tips: The name of the town of Pelayos de la Presa comes from San Pelayo, an old hermitage located between the current town and the San Esteban hill, while de la Presa takes its name from the stream that crosses its term.

There is a beach located in a place surrounded by vegetation where the Alberche river and the Perales river converge. In this place a sufficient amount of water is collected so that you can bathe calmly. You can also fish in some areas.

Incredible place full of antennas and radio telescopes to listen to space. The antennas impress, for their size and for the work they do. Check their official website to see if the museum is open or not.
You cannot miss the opportunity to visit the Safari Madrid animal park on the farm called El Rincón, where you can observe the animals in a state of semi-freedom, without cages.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Villa del Prado, Pelayos de la Presa, Aldea del Fresno circular route?
faqs.answer: While komoot estimates the cycling time to be around 3 hours and 10 minutes, this is a difficult 65.4 km route with 918 meters of elevation gain. For riders with very good fitness, it's more realistic to plan for a ride lasting over 5 hours, especially if you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is this racebike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as 'difficult' and a 'Hard road ride' requiring 'Very good fitness'. It is not recommended for beginners due to its significant length and elevation gain.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this racebike route?
faqs.answer: The route is mostly well-paved and easy to ride, which is ideal for a racebike. However, given its difficulty and length, some segments might be unpaved or more challenging, potentially requiring cyclists to dismount and push their bikes for short sections.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and landmarks can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You can expect spectacular and diverse landscapes throughout the Community of Madrid. Notable points include Finca Las Tejoneras, Alto de La Mira, Mirador del Conde de Martín, the Picadas Reservoir, and the Río Alberche Greenway. You'll also pass through the Pelayos de la Presa Town Center, near the Madrid Deep Space Communications Complex, and Safari Madrid (El Rincón Estate).

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: To enjoy the diverse landscapes and avoid extreme temperatures, spring and autumn are generally the best times for cycling in this region. Summers can be very hot, and winters might bring colder, less predictable weather.

faqs.question: Where does the route start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: As a circular route connecting Villa del Prado, Pelayos de la Presa, and Aldea del Fresno, you can start in any of these towns. Parking is generally available in the town centers of Villa del Prado, Pelayos de la Presa, or Aldea del Fresno, though specific cyclist parking areas may vary. It's advisable to check local parking regulations upon arrival.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it includes sections of the 'De Valencia a Lisboa (Etapa desde San Lorenzo del Escorial a Pelayos de la Presa)' and the 'Vía Verde del Alberche'.

faqs.question: Is this route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a racebike route primarily on roads. While dogs are generally allowed in public spaces, it's not typically considered a dog-friendly cycling route due to the speed and nature of road cycling. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure they are safely secured in a trailer designed for cycling and check local regulations for any specific restrictions.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this public road route connecting Villa del Prado, Pelayos de la Presa, and Aldea del Fresno.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for refreshments or accommodation along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through the towns of Villa del Prado, Pelayos de la Presa, and Aldea del Fresno, you will find various cafes, pubs, and restaurants where you can stop for refreshments. Accommodation options are also available in these towns if you plan to extend your stay.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a challenging ride like this?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, it's crucial to bring sufficient water and snacks, a repair kit for your racebike, appropriate cycling attire, sun protection, and a fully charged phone. A GPS device or the komoot app with the route downloaded is also highly recommended for navigation.
